title: Strong Erdős-Szemerédi Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: For every $c > 0$ and $\varepsilon > 0$, there is a threshold, $n_0$, such that if $n \geq n_0$ then for any $n$-element subset of reals $A \subset \mathbb{R}$ and any graph $G_n$ with $n$ vertices and at least $n^{1+c}$ edges $$|A +_{G_n} A| + |A \cdot_{G_n} A| \geq |A|^{1+c-\varepsilon}.$$
context_text: Erdős and Szemerédi introduced the sum-product problem along the edges of a graph. The Strong Erdős-Szemerédi Conjecture, stated here as Conjecture 1, was refuted in the authors' earlier paper [1]. The present paper describes improved constructions showing how far below the conjectured bound the sum and product sets along graph edges can be.
paper_contribution: Provides improved constructions (using real numbers rather than integers) giving counterexamples covering all edge densities in the range $0 \leq \alpha \leq 1/5$ (Theorem 2) and, by scaling, all $0 \leq \nu \leq 3/5$ (Theorem 3), yielding $|A +_{G_n} A| + |A \cdot_{G_n} A| = \tilde{O}(|A|^{2-4\alpha})$ for graphs with $\tilde{\Omega}(|A|^{2-2\alpha})$ edges.
notes: PDF extraction garbled 'c > 0' as 'c >' in the theorem header; reconstructed to 'c > 0' from context. Conjecture was already refuted in the authors' earlier paper [1]; this paper extends the refutation with improved constructions.
paper_title: On sums and products along the edges, II

title: Erdős-Szemerédi Matching Sum-Product Conjecture (within Problem 5)
statement_text: For two $n$-element sets of integers $A = \{a_1, \ldots, a_n\}$ and $B = \{b_1, \ldots, b_n\}$ with $S = \{a_i + b_i \mid 1 \leq i \leq n\}$ and $P = \{a_i \cdot b_i \mid 1 \leq i \leq n\}$, $$|P| + |S| = \Omega(n^{1/2+c})$$ for some constant $c > 0$.
context_text: Problem 5 frames the matching variant of the sum-product problem where sums and products are taken along a perfect matching between two $n$-element integer sets. Erdős and Szemerédi conjectured a super-$\sqrt{n}$ lower bound; the best unconditional result (Chang) gives $n^{1/2}\log^{1/48} n$, while Shkredov–Solymosi (Theorem 6) achieve $c = 1/10$ conditionally under the Bombieri-Lang conjecture.
paper_contribution: Shows (via Theorem 4 / [1, Theorem 3]) that for the multiset matching version $|P| + |S| = \tilde{O}(n^{4/5})$ is achievable, implying that Theorem 6's exponent $1/2 + c$ cannot be improved beyond $1/2 + 1/5 = 7/10$ by this approach.
notes: Conjecture is embedded in the body of Problem 5 (labeled by the paper authors) rather than in a standalone conjecture environment; attributed to Erdős and Szemerédi in the surrounding text with the same reference [5] as Conjecture 1.
paper_title: On sums and products along the edges, II
